idea-plugin.name: Argustack
idea-plugin.description:
Kanban board inside your IDE where columns are Claude Code skills.

Drag a task card to a skill column — Claude Code executes the skill automatically on that task.

How it works

  • Cards = Markdown files in your project (one file = one task)
  • Columns = skills from .claude/skills/ (auto-discovered)
  • Drag a card to a column → Claude runs that skill with the task as context

Features

  • Visual pipeline: plan → implement → test → review → deploy
  • Workflows — group skills into named pipelines
  • Epics — organize tasks by feature area
  • Done column time filter — show only recent completions
  • Jira integration — create tickets from the board
  • Terminal session persistence — resume interrupted Claude sessions
  • File system is the source of truth — board reflects your Docs/Tasks/ folder

Getting started

  1. Install Claude Code CLI
  2. Add skills to .claude/skills/ in your project
  3. Open the Argustack panel (right sidebar)
  4. Create a card, drag it to a skill column

Part of Argustack — open-source project analysis platform.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
v0.2.4

  • Skill execution fix — file stays stable during skill-to-skill drags
  • Context injection — plugin tells skill about card source and file state
  • Card redesign — colored epic badges, clean visual hierarchy
  • README auto-update — regenerates when plugin version changes
  • Done column time filter
